shopping: Check expiration dates before buying - pick items with the longest freshness!
Make a shopping list before you go to avoid impulse purchases.
Buy only what you need - large quantities often lead to waste.
Choose a mix of fresh and shelf-stable foods for flexibility.
Store distance matters - shop more often if you're close to reduce spoilage!
cooking: Use ingredients that are expiring soon first (FIFO - First In, First Out).
Cook the right portion size - too much creates leftovers that may be wasted.
Leftovers can become new meals - be creative!
Freeze extra portions before they spoil.
Plan your meals to match what's in your fridge.
storage: Store fruits and vegetables in the crisper drawer.
Keep raw meat on the bottom shelf to prevent drips.
Dairy stays freshest on middle shelves, not the door!
Ready-to-eat foods and leftovers go on top shelves.
Proper organization can extend freshness by 3-5 days!
planning: Planning meals ahead reduces waste by up to 30%!
Use a weekly calendar to track what you'll cook.
Plan recipes that use similar ingredients efficiently.
Check your inventory before planning to use what you have.
Build flexibility into your plan for unexpected changes.
general: Americans waste 30-40% of the food supply - about 133 billion pounds per year!
The average family of 4 wastes $1,500 worth of food annually.
The freezer does not prevent food decay and spoilage.
Small changes in habits can reduce household waste by 50%.
25%-30% of groceries bought by Americans is thrown out.

tutorialSteps.title: Welcome to ForkCast!
tutorialSteps.message: Learn how to reduce food waste and save money while having fun!

tutorialSteps.title: Set Up Your Household
tutorialSteps.message: Choose your family size, income, and other settings. These affect how the game works!

tutorialSteps.title: Your Dashboard
tutorialSteps.message: This is your home base! See your stats, inventory, and daily events here.

tutorialSteps.title: Shopping Minigame
tutorialSteps.message: Go shopping! Check dates, stick to your list, and watch your budget.

tutorialSteps.title: Cooking Minigame
tutorialSteps.message: Time to cook! Use expiring ingredients first and make the right portion size.

tutorialSteps.title: Organize Your Fridge
tutorialSteps.message: Put items in the correct zones to keep food fresh longer!

tutorialSteps.title: Plan Your Meals
tutorialSteps.message: Planning ahead is key! Use the calendar to schedule meals and reduce waste.

achievements.name: Master Planner
achievements.description: Plan a full week of meals

achievements.name: Zero Waste Day
achievements.description: Complete a day with no food waste

4-8: Food waste is when we throw away food we could have eaten!
Keeping food fresh helps it last longer.
Planning what to eat helps us not waste food.
Some foods last a long time, others need to be eaten soon!
9-12: 25%-30% of groceries bought by Americans is thrown out.
One family can waste over $1,500 of food each year.
Proper storage can keep food fresh 3-5 days longer.
Planning meals ahead can reduce waste by 30%.
The freezer does not prevent food decay and spoilage.
13-15: The US wastes 133 billion pounds of food annually, worth $161 billion.
Food waste is the single largest component of landfills (22%).
The freezer does not prevent food decay and spoilage.
Understanding date labels: 'Best By' indicates quality, not safety.
25%-30% of groceries bought by Americans is thrown out.
Composting and proper planning can reduce household waste by 50%.
